steps.title: Identify the land composition and building percentages
steps.content: The parcel is 56.0% farmland and 44.0% pasture. Buildings occupy 22.5% of the farmland and 15.0% of the pasture. To find the total percentage $p$ with buildings, we need to account for buildings in each region separately, then combine them.

steps.title: Calculate buildings on farmland as a percentage of the total plot
steps.content:
Buildings on farmland = 22.5% of farmland = 22.5% of 56.0% of the total parcel.
$$0.225 \times 0.560 = 0.1260$$
This equals 12.60% of the total parcel.

steps.title: Calculate buildings on pasture as a percentage of the total plot
steps.content:
Buildings on pasture = 15.0% of pasture = 15.0% of 44.0% of the total parcel.
$$0.150 \times 0.440 = 0.0660$$
This equals 6.60% of the total parcel.

steps.title: Add the two portions to find the total building percentage
steps.content:
The total percentage of the parcel with buildings is:
$$p = 12.60\% + 6.60\% = 19.20\%$$
Therefore, $p = 19.2$.
